6. PROBATION PERIOD, CONFIRMATION AND TERMINATION

giving one month no-ce or salary in lieu thereof on either side during this proba-on period with no liability

and data which cannot be fulﬁlled in the span of days.

cause' such as

indiscipline/ absence from duty without permission or any other conduct considered by the  
Company as detrimental to its interest or of viola-on of the condi-ons of service, or

11. CONFIDENTIALITY AND INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS  
11.1 You agree that the terms and condi-ons applying to your employment are strictly conﬁden-al. Any  
disclosure of these terms and condi-ons to third par-es (including other employees of the Company or any  
other associate or aﬃliate of the Company) cons-tutes a breach of your employment.  
11.2 You acknowledge that as a result of employment with the Company, you will be in possession of proprietary  
and conﬁden-al informa-on and trade secrets rela-ng to the business prac-ces of the Company and aﬃliated  
companies, if any. You agree that you will not, at any -me during or a\er the employment period, directly or  
indirectly, use or disclose to any person, ﬁrm, corpora-on or other en-ty, use to the detriment of the Company  
or the Company's employees or misuse in any way any proprietary or conﬁden-al informa-on acquired by you  
during your employment by Employer regarding the clients, customers, vendors, business partners or business  
prac-ces of Employer or aﬃliated companies, without the prior wriMen consent of the Company. For purposes  
of this Agreement, Conﬁden-al Informa-on includes, but is not limited to, trade secrets; lists of past or present  
clients, customers or consultants; product or service development plans; marke-ng plans; pricing policies;  
business acquisi-on plans or targets; any por-on or phase of any technical informa-on, technique, method,  
process, procedure, technology or know -how (whether or not in wriMen on tangible form) used by the Company  
or any por-on or phase of any technical informa-on, ideas, discoveries, designs, computer programs, processes,  
procedures, formulae or improvements of the Company that is valuable (whether or not in wriMen or tangible  
form or whether or not downloaded into a computer or on computer discs) to the Company. All such informa-on,  
in whatever form, including all memoranda, notes, plans, reports, records, documents and other evidence  
thereof and any other informa-on of whatever nature which gives the Employer an opportunity to obtain an  
advantage over its compe-tors shall be considered a "trade secret" for the purposes of this Agreement.

ANNEXURE B  
Code of Conduct  
This following sec-on of Policies and Procedures contains our rules and values, which deﬁne  
and specify the obliga-ons of all employees towards the Company, the work they perform  
and their behaviour and conduct within the workplace.  
This sec-on contains rules governing individual workplace conduct and speciﬁes, as broadly  
as possible, what the Company regards as misdemeanours and misconduct - both serious  
and moderate.  
A. POLICY  
1.  
The primary purpose of our Disciplinary Policy and Procedures - which includes among  
others the Business Code of Conduct, Company Code of Conduct, the Individual Code of  
Conduct and our workplace Safety and Security Code - is to ensure that all employees  
receive fair, unbiased and consistent treatment in the maintenance of discipline.  
2.  
The Company discipline is correc-ve in nature - except where it warrants dismissal in  
terms of common law, on grounds of accumula-on of past oﬀences or commiꢀng of a  
serious oﬀence.  
3.The maintenance of discipline through the enforcement of uniform standards, as well as  
the decision to ins-tute disciplinary proceedings, is the responsibility of the management.  
The Company expects employees to share informa-on about incidents of misconduct with  
the management.  
4.  
This Disciplinary Code and Procedure is part of the contract of employment and  
Condi-ons of Service for all Company employees.  
5.  
As prac-ces and aꢀtudes in the Company change with -me, and norms of acceptable  
conduct and performance evolve, this Code and Procedure, as well as the Schedule of  
oﬀences, is intended as a guideline and not as a rigid set of standards covering all acts of  
misconduct that may aMract disciplinary ac-on.  
6.  
Some units within the Company may require special disciplinary rules and codes of  
conduct to maintain eﬃciency and uniform standards of conduct in that par-cular division.  
Where this is the case, further rules and codes may be issued within the unit or department  
and shall apply to all employees or groups of employees working there. In these instances,  
such rules shall apply over and above the rules contained in the Disciplinary Code and  
www.neuzenai.com contact@neuzenai.com +91-8885257422  
NEUZENAI  
NEUZENAI IT Solutions Pvt Ltd  
Procedure – as long as they are within the scope of this policy manual and applicable labour  
legisla-on(s).  
7.  
It is the responsibility of every Company employee to ensure that he/she is familiar  
with the contents of this Disciplinary Code and that it is adhered to. Claiming of non-  
familiarity with the Company's disciplinary rules and procedures, will not serve as a defence  
for an employee.  
8.  
Managers and supervisors fulﬁl an important role as mentors and leaders. Their  
responsibility is to set an example to those whom they manage. Rela-ve seniority of an  
employee may therefore be an aggrava-ng factor where disciplinary sanc-ons involve  
those levels of staﬀ.  
9.  
Not all conduct that could cons-tute grounds for invoking this Disciplinary Code and  
Procedure necessarily occurs on Company premises or during working hours. Where the  
employee is charged under such circumstances, the Company shall take necessary ac-on  
as per the Code.  
10. The misdemeanours, misconduct and oﬀences men-oned in all four sec-ons of our  
Disciplinary Code and Procedures are not the only ones, and do not cover any and every  
incident of conduct that may lead to correc-ve or puni-ve ac-on by the Company.  
11. The headings given to sec-ons of this Disciplinary Procedure are to make for ease of  
use and reference to only and should not be used to interpret the contents of any sec-on  
of this Procedure.  
B. DISCIPLINE – THE ROLE OF MANAGEMENT  
1. Disciplinary ac-on should be correc-ve, the aim being to bring about a change in the  
behaviour of employees who have indulged in undesirable ac-ons so that they adhere  
willingly, through greater acceptance and understanding, to standards of conduct and  
performance.  
2. Puni-ve ac-on should only be taken when earlier correc-ve ac-on did not work – or when  
an oﬀence is so serious that the rela-onship of trust between employer and employee is  
destroyed.  
3. Repe--ve oﬀences commiMed in similar circumstances may result in a stronger ac-on  
being taken by the management.  
4. Oﬀences: Oﬀences can be divided into two categories namely:  
www.neuzenai.com contact@neuzenai.com +91-8885257422  
NEUZENAI  
NEUZENAI IT Solutions Pvt Ltd  
4.1 Serious oﬀences: These could, subject to an enquiry, result in employees being  
instantly dismissed.  
4.2 Moderate oﬀences: These usually consist of breaches of general discipline, which  
result in disciplinary ac-on.  
4.3 General terms  
(a) All disciplinary warnings will be cumula-ve for the current period,  
irrespec-ve of the previous misdemeanour for which disciplinary steps  
has been taken. The chairperson of an enquiry may, however, use his/her  
discre-on over the extent to which the alleged breach is related to or  
similar to previous conduct, for which disciplinary sanc-on was taken.  
(b) The role of the Human Resources Department will be purely one of  
consul-ng, monitoring and advising on procedural aspects of the  
disciplinary enquiry.  
C. GRIEVANCE POLICY  
1. A grievance is any complaint, dissa-sfac-on or feeling of injus-ce in connec-on with  
an employee’s work and employment situa-on that is brought to the aMen-on of  
the management.  
2. Grievances must be recorded in wri-ng by the aggrieved party and addressed to the  
department manager to whom he/she reports. Corporate Human Resources should  
receive a copy of the wriMen grievance to monitor its speedy resolu-on.  
3. A grievance is invalid if it is lodged by the employee in retalia-on against any  
disciplinary sanc-on received.  
4. Grievances may be limited to individuals or shared collec-vely. The nature of a  
grievance may vary according to the circumstances involved.  
5. The department manager handling the grievance, must try to resolve it as speedily  
and fairly as possible.  
6. If the grievance is not resolved within 3 days a\er being received by the department  
manager to whom it is addressed, this should be recorded in wri-ng.  
7. The grievance is then referred to the next level of management. h. If the aggrieved  
employee is le\ dissa-sﬁed a\er this further step, the procedure is repeated.  
www.neuzenai.com contact@neuzenai.com +91-8885257422  
NEUZENAI  
NEUZENAI IT Solutions Pvt Ltd  
8. Reasons for non-resolu-on are recorded in wri-ng and forwarded to successively  
higher levels of management, un-l the issue has been solved to the sa-sfac-on of  
all par-es involved.  
9. Corporate Human Resources will monitor all stages of the grievance procedure, and  
advise line managers, the aggrieved employee and his/her representa-ve on all  
aspects of correct procedure.  
D. INDIVIDUAL CODE OF CONDUCT  
1. As the Company places great value on the maintenance of good interpersonal  
rela-onships at the workplace between all employees, the following guidelines  
apply to the conduct of all individuals at all levels of the organiza-on.  
2. All employees are always expected to show mutual respect towards each other,  
regardless of their posi-on in the organiza-on.  
3. The Company places high value on the lack of discrimina-on of any kind in its  
corporate culture and therefore supports the values embodied in the country’s  
cons-tu-on and labour statutes to the fullest extent.  
4. Harmonious working rela-onships are important. Those who indulge in any form of  
insul-ng behaviour towards their colleagues, will be subject to serious disciplinary  
sanc-on.  
5. Vic-miza-on of any employee will not be tolerated and serious disciplinary ac-on  
for will be taken if the oﬀence is proved.  
6. Any unprovoked physical aMack or assault on a fellow employee will be subject to  
criminal process of the law of the land, as well as serious disciplinary ac-on by the  
management.  
7. Any form of direct or implied sexual harassment on the part of any employee  
towards any individual will lead to serious disciplinary ac-on.  
8. Use of Access Card: Employees should use their access cards properly and carry their  
access cards at all -mes.  
www.neuzenai.com contact@neuzenai.com +91-8885257422  
NEUZENAI  
NEUZENAI IT Solutions Pvt Ltd  
9. Tailga-ng is not allowed and punching someone else’s cards is a serious oﬀence  
which may result in disciplinary sanc-on.  
10. Employees’ aMendance will be monitored through the hours logged in using the  
access card.  
11. The above should serve as warning against any aMempts to disrupt the Company’s  
believe in the maintenance of professional and harmonious rela-onships at  
workplace.  
E. COMPANY CODE OF CONDUCT  
1. GUIDELINES: POOR PERFORMANCE, INCAPACITY  
1.1 An employee may be dismissed for reasons other than misconduct. The following  
instances of disciplinary ac-on culminate in dismissals that may not necessarily be  
puni-ve but relate to the eﬀec-ve opera-on of the business.  
2. POOR PERFORMANCE  
2.1 The proba-onary period of employment provides an opportunity for managers and new  
employees to assess whether the person suits the job and the job suits the person.  
2.2 Performance management is not conﬁned to the proba-onary period of new  
employees, -and the same steps should be followed regardless how long the employee  
has been employed.  
2.3 An employee should not be dismissed for unsa-sfactory performance unless the  
employer has:  
(a) given the employee appropriate evalua-on, instruc-on, training, guidance or  
counselling; and  
(b) a\er a reasonable period of -me for improvement, the employee con-nues to  
perform unsa-sfactorily.  
2.4 The procedure leading to dismissal should include an inves-ga-on to establish the  
reasons or the unsa-sfactory performance and the employer should consider other  
ways, short of dismissal, to remedy the maMer.  
www.neuzenai.com contact@neuzenai.com +91-8885257422  
NEUZENAI  
NEUZENAI IT Solutions Pvt Ltd  
2.5 Any person determining whether a dismissal for poor work performance is unfair,  
should consider - whether or not the employee failed to meet a performance standard;  
and if the employee did not meet a required performance standard; and if the employee  
was aware, or could reasonably be expected to have been aware, of the required  
performance standard; was  
(a) the employee was given a fair opportunity to meet the required performance  
standard; and  
(b) dismissal is an appropriate sanc-on for not mee-ng the required performance  
standard.  
3 Incapacity: Ill health or injury  
3.1 Incapacity on the grounds of ill health or injury may be temporary or permanent. If an  
employee is temporarily unable to work in these circumstances, the employer should  
inves-gate the extent of the incapacity or the injury. If the employee is likely to be absent  
for a -me that is unreasonably long in the circumstances, the employer should inves-gate  
all possible alterna-ves, short of dismissal. When alterna-ves are considered, relevant  
factors might include the nature of the job, the period of absence, the seriousness of the  
illness or injury and the possibility of securing a temporary replacement for the ill or injured  
employee. In cases of permanent incapacity, the employer should ascertain the possibility  
of securing alterna-ve employment or adap-ng the employee’s du-es or work  
circumstances to accommodate his/her disability.  
3.2 In the process of the inves-ga-on referred to in subsec-on (1), the employee should  
be allowed the opportunity to state his/her case.  
3.3 The degree of incapacity is relevant to the fairness or any dismissal. The cause of the  
incapacity may also be relevant.  
3.4 Par-cular considera-on should be given to employees who are injured at work or who  
are incapacitated by work-related illness.  
3.5 Guidelines in cases of dismissal arising from ill health or injury. In determining whether  
a dismissal arising from ill health or injury is unfair, it should be considered:  
(a) whether or not the employee is capable of performing the work; and  
(b) If the employee is not capable, what is –  
www.neuzenai.com contact@neuzenai.com +91-8885257422  
NEUZENAI  
NEUZENAI IT Solutions Pvt Ltd  
(i)  
the extent to which the employee is able to perform the work;  
(ii)  
the extent to which the employee's work circumstances might be  
adapted to accommodate his/her disability, or, where this is not possible, the extent  
to which the employee's du-es might be adapted; and  
(iii) the availability of any other suitable alterna-ve work.  
3.6 As in other cases of progressive or correc-ve disciplinary ac-on, line management  
must prove, through documented records, that the correct procedures have been followed  
and that the dismissed employee received fair treatment.  
4. SCHEDULE OF OFFENCES – COMPANY CODE OF CONDUCT  
4.1 Serious Oﬀences: These may lead to a ﬁnal wriMen warning being issued or could,  
subject to an enquiry, result in summary dismissal. These examples are not the only  
oﬀences and serve only as an illustra-on:  
(a) The\, bribery, fraud, dishonesty or any related oﬀences as listed in the Business  
Code of Conduct.  
(b) Falsiﬁca-on of the employers’ records.  
(c) Misuse of the Company’s property for private purposes (this being the\)  
(d) Gross negligence or incompetence.  
(e) Making false statements or misrepresenta-on when applying for employment.  
(f) Absence from the workplace while on duty (depending on the nature of the job,  
e.g. an employee whose absence will hold up the work of other employees and  
cause serious prejudice to the Company).  
(g) Unauthorized absenteeism.  
(h) Wilful damage to the Company’s equipment, or the property of other employees or  
that of clients.  
(i) Figh-ng, assault, or aMempted assault.  
www.neuzenai.com contact@neuzenai.com +91-8885257422  
NEUZENAI  
NEUZENAI IT Solutions Pvt Ltd  
(j) Being drunk or under the inﬂuence of illegal drugs during working hours.  
(k) Causing damage to the Company’s property through drunkenness or serious  
neglect.  
(l) Sabotage by damaging machinery – or causing damage to the Company’s property  
in any way whatsoever.  
(m) Illegal striking or inﬂuencing others to strike illegally.  
(n) Refusal to obey reasonable work-related instruc-ons given to the employees by a  
manager or supervisor designated by the employer.  
(o) Failure to report misconduct of other employees.  
(p) Deser-on.  
(q) Persistent misconduct  
4.2 Moderate Oﬀences: These usually consist of breaches of general discipline, resul-ng in  
disciplinary ac-on. The below are not the only possibili-es and serve only for  
illustra-on.  
(a) Laziness, loaﬁng – passing -me idly or failing to complete tasks set without  
reasonable cause.  
(b) Poor workmanship/ineﬃciency; failure to carry out work to the required standard  
without reasonable cause and concealing defec-ve work.  
(c) Poor -me keeping, e.g. arriving late or leaving early.  
(d) Disorderly behaviour on employer’s premises.  
(e) Negligence: negligent loss, damage or misuse of Company property; failure to  
exercise proper care in execu-ng du-es to the extent that tasks have to be  
repeated.  
(f) Being disrespecxul, rude and uncoopera-ve towards clients, fellow employees and  
management.  
www.neuzenai.com contact@neuzenai.com +91-8885257422  
NEUZENAI  
NEUZENAI IT Solutions Pvt Ltd  
5. Disciplinary sanc-ons  
5.1 There are four basic methods of disciplinary ac-on that can be taken against an  
employee. In order of severity these are:  
(a) Verbal warning  
(b) WriMen warning  
(c) Final wriMen warning  
(d) Dismissal with pay in lieu of no-ce or summary dismissal.  
6
Disciplinary procedure  
6.1 Disciplinary enquiries are not convened for allega-ons that aMract the following  
disciplinary sanc-ons:  
(a) Verbal warnings  
(i)  
In the case of a moderate oﬀence, a manager or supervisor should  
conduct an informal disciplinary interview with the employee that may  
result in a verbal reprimand.  
(ii)  
The supervisor or manager should keep a wriMen record of any verbal  
warnings issued to his employees, strictly for his/her own records -  
no-ng date, -me of informal disciplinary interview, a brief descrip-on  
of what was said and the reason why the verbal reprimand was given.  
(b) WriMen Warnings  
(i)  
If the verbal warnings fail, or the oﬀences grow more serious, the supervisor  
or manager should give the employee a formal wriMen warning. WriMen  
record of this is kept and noted on the employee’s record and it is valid for a  
period of three months.  
(c) Final WriMen Warning  
(i)  
A repe--on of wrongful behaviour (or if a more serious oﬀence/misconduct  
is commiMed) can result in a ﬁnal wriMen warning. A ﬁnal wriMen warning is  
valid for a period of six months, wherea\er the employee will revert to a  
clear record (an excep-on is dishonest conduct, where the speciﬁc nature  
and circumstances will be taken into account).  
(ii)  
All wriMen and ﬁnal wriMen warnings should be recorded in the form of a  
leMer of no-ﬁca-on to the employee and placed on the employee’s record.  
www.neuzenai.com contact@neuzenai.com +91-8885257422  
NEUZENAI  
NEUZENAI IT Solutions Pvt Ltd  
A copy of the signed leMer is handed to the employee during a discussion  
between the employee and his/her line manager or supervisor. Line  
managers/supervisors may request a representa-ve from Corporate Human  
Resources to be present.  
7.  
Dismissal with pay in lieu of no-ce or summary dismissal  
7.1  
Dismissal, or the possible dismissal of any employee, cannot take place without  
convening a full enquiry into the circumstances surrounding the alleged serious  
oﬀence.  
7.2  
When, in the employer’s opinion:  
(a) series of performance improvement measures have not produced the an-cipated  
eﬀect; or  
(b) a series of verbal or wriMen reprimands/warnings given for minor misconduct have  
not been eﬀec-ve; or  
(c) if an employee is alleged to have commiMed major misconduct, the Company  
should, before taking disciplinary ac-on, hold a formal disciplinary enquiry.  
8.  
Formal disciplinary enquiry  
8.1  
The following principles should be observed at the enquiry:  
(a) The enquiry should be held as soon as possible a\er the event, provided that the  
employee is given a reasonable -me (minimum 48 hours) to prepare his/her  
defence;  
(b) The employee may, if necessary, be suspended on full pay prior to, during or pending  
the outcome of the enquiry;  
(c) The employee should, within a reasonable -me a\er the alleged oﬀence was  
commiMed, be no-ﬁed in wri-ng of the date, -me and venue of the enquiry, the  
misconduct which he/she is alleged to have commiMed and of his/her rights at the  
enquiry.  
9.  
Conduc-ng the formal disciplinary enquiry  
Normally present at the enquiry are:  
9.1  
www.neuzenai.com contact@neuzenai.com +91-8885257422  
NEUZENAI  
NEUZENAI IT Solutions Pvt Ltd  
(a) The chairperson. In terms of fair prac-ce, the chairperson should be at the same  
level, or higher, than the complainant and as far removed from all allega-ons and  
issues involved in the enquiry as possible.  
(b) The accused. This is the employee against whom the allega-ons for serious  
misconduct have been made.  
(c) An employee representa-ve unless the accused does not require representa-on.  
The representa-ve should be a fellow employee, as no outside representa-on is  
allowed.  
(d) The complainant. This is usually the accused’s manager, whose role it is to  
inves-gate the serious misconduct and formulate the charges against the accused.  
(e) A representa-ve from Human Resources, whose duty it is to coordinate and arrange  
the enquiry.  
9.2  
9.3  
When the enquiry starts, the chairperson should read out and explain the  
misconduct under enquiry. The chairperson should ask the accused if he/she  
understands the complaint and if so whether he/she pleads guilty or not guilty.  
The employee’s rights at the inquiry are to:  
(a) have representa-on by a fellow employee, if requested.  
(b) have the opportunity to confer with the representa-ve, at reasonable -me  
before, during and a\er the inquiry.  
(c) ques-on the complainant and witness during the inquiry either himself or  
through his representa-ve.  
(d) give evidence himself/herself (he/she cannot be compelled to do so); to call  
witnesses to give evidence and to argue either himself/herself or through  
his/her representa-ve on the ques-on of whether the misconduct occurred.  
(e) give evidence himself/herself; to call witnesses to give evidence and to argue  
either or through his/her representa-ve in mi-ga-on of disciplinary ac-on.  
9.4  
A\er hearing all sides of the case, the chairperson must decide whether the alleged  
misconduct was commiMed or not and if so, on the appropriate sanc-on to be taken.  
www.neuzenai.com contact@neuzenai.com +91-8885257422  
NEUZENAI  
NEUZENAI IT Solutions Pvt Ltd  
9.5  
The appropriate sanc-on will take into account the circumstances surrounding the  
misconduct, the seriousness of the misconduct, whether or not the misconduct has  
destroyed the employment rela-onship, and which sanc-ons are in line with current  
Company policy.  
9.6  
9.7  
Sanc-ons should be in line with the employer’s policy and thus with previous  
decisions involving the same or substan-ally the same circumstances.  
The outcome of the inquiry may include –  
(a) Exonera-on  
(b) Final wriMen warning  
(c) Dismissal with pay in lieu of no-ce  
(d) Summary dismissal  
9.8  
The chairperson must not consult the employee’s previous work records un-l a\er  
he has reached a decision on whether or not the alleged serious misconduct was  
commiMed.  
10.  
Criminal oﬀences  
10.1  
11  
The Company will, in its discre-on, press criminal charges against any employee  
where this is warranted by the nature of the alleged misconduct.  
Right to Appeal  
11.1. An employee has the right to appeal against any sanc-ons, to be heard by a person  
appointed by the employer. This appeal must be lodged in the oﬃcial form within 5 working  
days of the decision. An appeal can be held in the form of a full re-hearing of the maMer, or  
it can take the form of a review of the disciplinary enquiry. The form that the appeal will  
take, will be at the discre-on of the chairperson of the appeal hearing and will depend on  
the reasons for the appeal. An employee is not en-tled to rec-fy any defect whether  
procedural or otherwise at the appeal hearing.  
F. LEAVE  
1. You will be eligible to the beneﬁts of the Company’s Leave Policy on your  
conﬁrma-on in the Company’s Service.  
www.neuzenai.com contact@neuzenai.com +91-8885257422  
NEUZENAI  
NEUZENAI IT Solutions Pvt Ltd  
2. Absence for a con-nuous period of ten days without prior approval of your superior,  
(including overstay on leave / training) would result in your losing your lien on the  
service and the same shall automa-cally come to an end without any no-ce or  
in-ma-on.  
3. You shall abide by the rules and regula-ons of the Company as contained in this  
LeMer of Employment and as informed to you by the Company from -me to -me.  
With the acceptance of this employment, you agree and conﬁrm that during your  
employment, you will be governed by the Company’s Policies, as amended from  
-me to -me. The policies are available on the Public Folder of all systems and should  
be read and understood by you. In the event of any clariﬁca-on w.r.t the same please  
contact the HR Department.
